evals.prompt: I just committed a fix for the token parser crash on empty input. Create a PR for it.
evals.expected_output: Single-line PR title matching the commit subject, no body, presented for confirmation before creation
evals.context: Branch has exactly one commit ahead of main: 'fix(parser): handle empty input without panic'. No PR template in repo.

evals.prompt: Open a PR for this branch. I've been working on adding OAuth support.
evals.expected_output: Conventional commit title summarizing the overall change, 2-4 sentence motivation paragraph in body, Closes #312 footer, presented for confirmation
evals.context: Branch 'feat/312-oauth-support' has 4 commits: 'feat(auth): add OAuth provider config', 'feat(auth): implement token exchange middleware', 'feat(auth): add refresh token rotation', 'test(auth): add OAuth flow integration tests'. No PR template.

evals.prompt: Create a PR for my changes.
evals.expected_output: Conventional commit title, template used as skeleton with empty sections removed (Screenshots removed for backend-only change), minimal content in remaining sections, presented for confirmation
evals.context: Branch has 2 commits: 'refactor(api): extract validation into middleware' and 'feat(api): add rate limiting to public endpoints'. Repo has .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md with sections: ## Description, ## Testing, ## Screenshots, ## Breaking Changes.
